beats.say: Here's the key insight that makes projectile motion solvable: we can treat horizontal and vertical motions as completely separate problems. Watch as we split the velocity vector into independent components.

card.content: **Independence Principle**: Horizontal and vertical motions are independent — gravity affects only the vertical component.

beats.say: The horizontal component uses cosine. With our 30-degree angle, that's 40 times cosine of 30 degrees, which equals 40 times root 3 over 2.
The vertical component uses sine. At 30 degrees, sine equals one-half, so we get 40 times one-half, which is exactly 20 meters per second upward.
Now here's what makes this beautiful: gravity only affects vertical motion. It pulls the arrow downward but can't slow its horizontal movement. Let's see both motions side by side.

card.content: **Key Insight**: Gravity acts only vertically — the horizontal velocity stays constant at 34.6 m/s throughout the entire flight.
